course_objective: Introduce the fundamental concepts of embedded systems, including architecture, microcontrollers, and real-time system requirements.
Develop proficiency in writing and debugging C programs tailored for embedded systems, focusing on memory constraints and hardware interaction.
Gain hands-on experience interfacing with microcontroller peripherals such as GPIO, timers, ADCs, and communication protocols (UART, SPI, I2C).
Implement real-time embedded applications with emphasis on task scheduling, interrupt handling, and performance optimization.
Build, test, and deploy embedded projects integrating hardware and software, demonstrating the complete development lifecycle.

course_outcomes.description: Recall basic concepts of embedded systems and microcontroller architecture
Describe the syntax, structure, and features of Embedded C programming.
Explain how Embedded C interacts with hardware components like GPIO, timers, and interrupts
Apply Embedded C programming to control basic hardware peripherals such as LEDs, switches, and displays
Demonstrate the use of communication protocols (UART, SPI, I2C) in Embedded C applications
Analyze the functionality and performance of Embedded C programs through simulation and testing tools.

modules.title: Introduction to Embedded Systems
topics: Definition and characteristics of embedded systems
Differences between embedded and general-purpose systems
Classification and applications of embedded systems
Overview of microcontroller vs microprocessor
Embedded system development life cycle (EDLC)
Introduction to microcontroller architectures (e.g., 8051, AVR, PIC, ARM)

modules.title: Basics of Embedded C Programming
topics: Introduction to C language for embedded systems
Data types, variables, operators, and expressions
Control structures (if, switch, loops)
Functions, arrays, strings, and pointers
Bit manipulation techniques
Memory management in embedded C

modules.title: Microcontroller Programming Basics
topics: Microcontroller components: CPU, RAM, ROM, I/O ports
Architecture and pin configuration of a sample microcontroller (e.g., 8051 or ATmega328)
Programming tools: IDEs, compilers, and simulators
Writing and compiling first Embedded C program
Downloading code to microcontroller using programmer
LED blinking and basic I/O programming

modules.title: Interfacing Digital I/O Devices
topics: Interfacing switches, LEDs, buzzers
Interfacing 7-segment displays and LCD (16x2)
Introduction to keypad interfacing
Debouncing techniques and input polling
Using timers and counters for delays and measurement

modules.title: Interrupts and Communication Protocols
topics: Concept of interrupts and types of interrupts
Writing ISR (Interrupt Service Routines)
External and internal interrupts programming
Introduction to UART, SPI, and I2C protocols
Serial communication: data transfer and receiving
Sensor interfacing using I2C/SPI

modules.title: Embedded Systems Applications and Mini Project
topics: PWM generation and applications (e.g., motor control)
ADC (Analog to Digital Converter) interfacing
Real-time clock (RTC) integration
Introduction to RTOS basics (optional)
Mini project: Design, development, and demonstration of an embedded application (e.g., temperature display, home automation)

textbooks.title: Programming Embedded Systems in C and C++
textbooks.author: Michael Barr
